observations: Default route uses link-scoped gateway link#15, so next-hop reachability is less explicit.

findings.title: Default route exists but looks inconsistent with local interface state
findings.summary: A default route was collected, but route and interface evidence suggest it may not be usable.
evidence: Default route uses link-scoped gateway link#15, so next-hop reachability is less explicit.
Default route uses interface tun0, but that interface was not collected as active.
Default route uses interface tun0, but that interface has no usable non-loopback address.
findings.probable_cause: The route table contains a default path, but the host still appears locally misrouted, attached to the wrong interface, or missing a usable next-hop path.
findings.plain_language: The host has a default route on paper, but the route and interface evidence do not line up cleanly.
findings.evidence_summary: Default route uses link-scoped gateway link#15, so next-hop reachability is less explicit. Default route uses interface tun0, but that interface was not collected as active.
safe_next_actions: Verify the expected interface is still the active interface before rerunning.
If a dock, VPN, or secondary adapter recently changed state, reconnect it once.
escalation_triggers: Escalate if the default route still points to an inactive or unusable interface after the interface is reset.
uncertainty_notes: The route table inconsistency can indicate stale local state, policy routing, or an incomplete next-hop path.

findings.title: DNS resolution is only partially successful
findings.summary: Some configured hostnames resolved while others failed, which suggests selective DNS reachability or split-horizon behavior rather than a total DNS outage.
evidence: Failed DNS lookups: python.org (temporary failure in name resolution).
findings.probable_cause: Resolver selection, split-horizon DNS, or intermittent upstream resolver reachability may be affecting only part of the configured namespace set.
findings.plain_language: Some hostnames resolved and others did not, so DNS behavior is inconsistent rather than completely down.
findings.evidence_summary: Successful DNS lookups: github.com -> 140.82.114.3. Failed DNS lookups: python.org (temporary failure in name resolution).
safe_next_actions: Keep both public and environment-specific hostnames in the run so the split is visible.
If VPN is relevant, compare results with and without the tunnel connected only if policy allows it.
escalation_triggers: Escalate if the same namespace subset keeps failing after reconnecting the intended network path.
uncertainty_notes: Selective DNS failures can be caused by split-horizon DNS, resolver choice, or intermittent upstream reachability.

what_we_know: The host has a default route on paper, but the route and interface evidence do not line up cleanly.
likely_happened: The route table contains a default path, but the host still appears locally misrouted, attached to the wrong interface, or missing a usable next-hop path.
Heuristic conclusion: Resolver selection, split-horizon DNS, or intermittent upstream resolver reachability may be affecting only part of the configured namespace set.
safe_next_steps: Verify the expected interface is still the active interface before rerunning.
If a dock, VPN, or secondary adapter recently changed state, reconnect it once.
Keep both public and environment-specific hostnames in the run so the split is visible.
If VPN is relevant, compare results with and without the tunnel connected only if policy allows it.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the default route still points to an inactive or unusable interface after the interface is reset.
Escalate if the same namespace subset keeps failing after reconnecting the intended network path.
uncertainty_notes: The route table inconsistency can indicate stale local state, policy routing, or an incomplete next-hop path.
Selective DNS failures can be caused by split-horizon DNS, resolver choice, or intermittent upstream reachability.
Routing inventory was only partially collected or produced mixed results.
DNS checks was only partially collected or produced mixed results.
Generic connectivity was only partially collected or produced mixed results.
